latest_post_merge_uat.next_optimization_focus: consume embedded Vortex layout and string/domain metadata more deeply for high-cardinality aggregate, string predicate/grouping, and bounded top-K families; preserve one .vortex artifact, no query-answer sidecars, no fallback, and no external engine execution

command_shape: target/release/shardloom run dataframe --input <hits.vortex> --input-format vortex --execution-policy native_vortex --request collect --bounded true --format json

current_replacement_artifact_uat.artifact_shape_tradeoff: The adaptive 262144-row source-stream policy reduced record batches from 1526 to 382 and Vortex footer segments from 65527 to 33495 versus the first source-text success, but increased local prepare time from 834654ms to 950957ms. Treat this as retained single-artifact structure evidence, not a load-speed closeout.

optimization_families.strategy: row-reference/selection-vector top-K with prepared predicate and payload locality; final materialization only for retained rows
dictionary-aware exact distinct with dense IDs/bitsets where exact and encoded-key merge before decode
partitioned capillary partial state, packed typed/composite keys, memory-budgeted exact merge, and spill diagnostics for near-unique groups
embedded generic string/domain metadata, segment absence certificates, byte-length/domain dictionaries, and exact predicate masks before materialization

families.dominant_cost_class: exact distinct state rebuilt at query time
families.query_time_goal: merge exact prepared distinct state before decoded value materialization
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL COUNT(DISTINCT), Python/DataFrame nunique/value_counts/distinct, and CLI SQL all select the same native_vortex_unified_plan distinct contract

families.dominant_cost_class: large or near-unique group state rebuilt at query time
families.query_time_goal: consume exact prepared partial state and merge retained candidates instead of scanning all rows into one hot map
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL GROUP BY, Python/DataFrame group_by().agg(), nlargest/nsmallest, and CLI SQL all share the prepared aggregate/top-K route

families.dominant_cost_class: runtime string scan, host extraction, or string-key retention
families.query_time_goal: prune from prepared exact string metadata and group on encoded IDs before decoding retained output strings
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL LIKE/REGEXP_REPLACE/length and Python/DataFrame contains/replace/length/domain helpers all use the same prepared string route

families.dominant_cost_class: wide row payload reread or materialization before final retained row set
families.query_time_goal: retain row references through sort/top-K and materialize only final output rows
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL ORDER BY LIMIT/OFFSET, Python/DataFrame sort/head/tail/nlargest, and CLI SQL all share the same late-materialized top-N route

families.dominant_cost_class: derived expression recomputation across aggregate/update paths
families.query_time_goal: evaluate reusable exact derived columns once per prepared state or once per retained capillary unit
families.surface_reuse_requirement: SQL expressions and Python/DataFrame expression builders use the same expression fingerprint and derived-column admission path

prepared_olap_reuse_plan.query_time_consumption_status: implemented_whole_file_footer_pruning_for_count_filter_aggregate_and_bounded_sort_local_primitives_via_VortexFile_can_prune; deeper segment/string/domain metadata-first pruning, dictionary/layout policy, and broader ClickBench-heavy planner consumption remain planned runtime optimization work
prepared_olap_reuse_plan.shared_runtime_contract: Universal Ingest -> SourceState -> VortexPreparedState -> prepared_olap_state when available -> native_vortex_unified_plan -> typed result or sink
prepared_olap_reuse_plan.single_artifact_storage_model: one prepared .vortex artifact; no adjacent prepared-OLAP manifest, .prepared-olap-state.d directory, or query-summary sidecar is allowed on the public/default runtime path

rejected_prepared_olap_query_summary_sid.reason: Exact query-summary sidecars behave like query-contract warm state and are not the ShardLoom product path. Public/default runtime uses one prepared .vortex artifact with embedded/generic Vortex layout and statistics evidence; external prepared-OLAP manifests and sidecar directories are disabled.

targeted_current_guardrails.ingest_ship_drop_target: Current accepted local 100M Parquet replacement UAT verifies source-to-Vortex normalization, source-text dictionary-Zstd writer coverage, single .vortex artifact output, embedded OLAP/layout evidence, no external manifest/sidecar files, no hidden temp files after completion, and fallback/external-engine=false. Next ingest work is writer-profile tuning from this evidence: reduce vortex_segment_write_millis without losing the single-artifact/no-sidecar contract or increasing final artifact size materially.

rows.reason: Pure URL contains count remains dominated by full string scan work; this confirms no regression but does not close the string predicate family.
Mixed pushdown plus residual contains route remains correct and slightly faster, but still needs embedded string/domain metadata or segment pruning to move the dominant cost class.

latest_replacement_ingest_guard.next_action: Do not make a load-time claim from this attempt. Either run a safety-only CPU-throttled local check with no performance claim, adjust the local guard policy to allow one fully used core, or further redesign the writer/source pipeline before replacement UAT.

targeted_current_guardrails.open_conclusion: Q33 now has a retained two-pass numeric-pair late-measure route (14.86s targeted local UAT), and Q34/Q35 now have a retained proof-bound string count top-K heavy-hitter recount route (33.958s/34.064s, exact parity, no fallback/external engine). Remaining slow families are string predicate/grouping rows (CB-Q21-CB-Q29), high-cardinality minute/string groups (CB-Q19), exact distinct cleanup (CB-Q14 and related rows), and ingest/load throughput. These need embedded string/domain metadata, dictionary/layout consumption, spill/state-budget hardening, or source-side ingest improvements rather than query-specific answer caches.

rejected_changes_after_targeted_uat.reason: Maintaining the retained candidate window on every near-unique numeric-pair group update added hot-loop work and caused the 100M Q33 route to exceed the 180-second UAT cap; the runtime code path was removed before shipment.
Counting the FSST LIKE bool mask directly did not reduce the current artifact path and regressed the URL LIKE count row; deeper prepared string/domain metadata remains the required optimization.

Splitting the near-unique numeric-pair group map into fixed partitions added routing/merge overhead without reducing the dominant 99,997,493-group state volume; the code path was removed before shipment.

The candidate did not activate a faster route on the current Vortex artifact and the existing URLHash column is not an exact functional surrogate for URL grouping: a bounded sample found multiple URLHash values for the same URL. The runtime code and tests were removed before shipment. Future URL grouping work must use exact embedded domain/dictionary metadata or source-derived columns created during Universal Ingest with source-hash invalidation.

retained_changes.reason: The route now builds exact count state first, retains the bounded top-K numeric-pair keys, and reopens the same single .vortex artifact to materialize SUM/AVG measures only for retained keys. Targeted 100M UAT showed exact result-row parity with the prior one-pass route while reducing Q33 from the 127-173s range to 14.86s.

The route uses a generic exact proof-bound string top-K operator: first-pass weighted heavy-hitter candidate sketch over chunk UTF-8 dictionaries, lower-bound proof before exact recount, second-pass candidate-only recount on the same single .vortex artifact, and native exact fallback if proof is not possible. It preserved exact result-row parity for CB-Q34/CB-Q35 while reducing the current URL grouping path by roughly 63-66%.
The Vortex primitive decode probe still materialized most hot columns and was slower than the current numeric-pair path; the code path was removed before shipment.

Exact comparison, null, and IN-list residual predicates now select counts/row indices from projected Vortex columns through typed, dictionary, or materialized accessors and can feed exact candidate row indices directly into scalar/grouped aggregate accessors without selected-row export or predicate re-check. Offset-heavy count-star grouped top-K now switches from a linear retained-window scan to capillary select-nth retention when offset+limit exceeds 128, preserving deterministic final sort while avoiding O(groups*offset) candidate comparisons. Mixed residual predicates that are not fully exact still materialize and re-check candidates.

comparison_to_prior_source_text_success.conclusion: artifact shape improved materially, but prepare time regressed; keep load-speed tuning open.

previous_rejected_replacement_attempt.rejection_reason: bounded Capillary Arrow-to-Vortex normalization prefetch plus narrow text compression improved materially versus all-column balanced compression, but it still did not complete a 100M Parquet replacement within the three-minute local UAT cap. Temp output reached 2.54GB and was cleaned up; no artifact was retained.
previous_rejected_replacement_attempt.fix_after_rejection: Keep the bounded Capillary normalization-worker pipeline and replace the narrow writer shape with source-schema text dictionary-Zstd field policy before retaining a replacement artifact.

prior_rejected_replacement_attempts.rejection_reason: all-column balanced BtrBlocks/Zstd-compatible large-source writer profile made only 145845496 temp bytes after 191.341 seconds

bounded Capillary Arrow-to-Vortex normalization prefetch made 100M Parquet replacement ingest materially faster, but the uncompressed fast-load large-source writer profile ballooned the artifact to about 50GiB
ordered per-RecordBatch Parquet row-group emission alone did not materially improve 100M Parquet replacement ingest
retained custom 65,536-row / 1MiB large-source writer strategy was still too slow before source/writer overlap redesign
upstream-default writer substitution produced only about 46MiB temp output before stop and was slower than the retained custom writer path
default physical derived-column synthesis still made 100M Parquet replacement ingest progress too slowly after the writer/coalescer experiment was removed
physical Arrow batch coalescing plus a 262,144-row/8 MiB writer target was slower than the retained replacement-ingest pace

previous_rejected_replacement_attempt.next_action: Keep source-schema text dictionary-Zstd as the current single-artifact correctness baseline and continue writer-profile timing work before making any load-speed claim.
